note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Founder to review before wiring live.

quizzes.title: Introduction to Drying Health Care Facilities — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: Compared with residential HVAC equipment, what filter rating do hospital facilities commonly employ to improve indoor air quality and reduce infection risk?
options: MERV 6 to 8
MERV 1 to 2
MERV 13 or higher
MERV 20 to 25

questions.questionText: In a protected environment for immunocompromised patients, staged pressure differentials are maintained so that air flows in which direction?
options: From the construction zone into the patient wing
From the patient wing towards the construction zone
Equally in both directions to balance the space
No airflow is permitted between the areas

questions.questionText: Why do hospital wall assemblies use non-porous monolithic sheathing rather than the porous materials common in residential settings?
options: It absorbs moisture to help the structure dry faster
It is chosen mainly to reduce construction costs
It improves thermal insulation and nothing else
It prevents absorption of biological materials and allows repeated antimicrobial disinfection

questions.questionText: When restoring flood-affected medical gas infrastructure, which component should be prioritised first?
options: Exterior bulk tank farms
Nitrous oxide pipe chases
Central oxygen manifolds
All components are restored at the same priority
questions.sourceRef: M2 — Unique Building Systems (Medical Gas Infrastructure)

questions.questionText: To protect sterile pharmaceutical clean rooms from humidity infiltration, what air pressure relationship should be maintained relative to surrounding areas?
options: Negative air pressure
Positive air pressure
Neutral pressure with no gradient
Alternating positive and negative pressure

questions.questionText: How do sequentially staged containments help prevent cross-contamination during healthcare drying work?
options: They combine all activities into a single shared work zone for efficiency
They remove physical barriers so air can circulate freely
They rely solely on staff hand hygiene without designated zones
They establish distinct work zones separated by risk level, such as clean, intermediate and dirty activities
questions.sourceRef: M3 — Infection Control Risks (Cross Contamination Potentials)

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of dividing hospital wings into compartmentalised project bands during restoration?
options: To remove the need for regulatory inspections before reopening
To reduce the total number of contractors required on site
To allow staggered reopening of departments as they are cleared, preventing whole-scale disruption
To keep the entire hospital closed until all work is finished
questions.sourceRef: M4 — Maintaining Critical Operations (Staged Functionality Restoration)

questions.questionText: Before removing containment enclosures and returning a wing to occupancy, clearance verification testing should assess which hazards?
options: Only visible dust on surfaces
Lead dust particulates, VOC off-gassing and microbiological organisms
Only the room temperature and humidity
Only the noise levels from equipment
